archetype: Real-Time Ionization Chamber Drift & MAMI (Monitoring of Accelerator Magnetic fields) Correction Engine for Proton Therapy Beam Delivery
description: A deterministic control system that synchronizes cyclotron beam extraction with gantry rotation and patient positioning to correct for magnetic field hysteresis and ionization chamber drift in real-time during proton therapy. It is used by medical physicists and radiation oncologists to ensure sub-millimeter beam placement accuracy, requiring the fusion of high-frequency particle physics telemetry with low-latency mechanical actuation feedback loops under strict radiation safety interlocks.
rules: MUST implement IEC 60601-2-64 compliance for medical electrical equipment safety limits and fail-safe logic.
MUST process data via the DICOM RT Plan and DICOM RT Dose standards for beam trajectory mapping.
MUST sustain sub-100 microsecond control loops for beam modulation to prevent dose overshoot in healthy tissue.
MUST integrate with proprietary accelerator hardware protocols (e.g., VMEbus or CANopen for cyclotron control) without jitter.
MUST enforce a dual-channel redundancy architecture where independent logic modules must agree before beam activation is permitted.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use asynchronous event-driven architectures for the core safety interlock logic, as race conditions could result in lethal radiation dosage errors.
DO NOT rely on cloud-based latency for beam control decisions; the system must operate entirely on a localized, air-gapped real-time kernel.
DO NOT use probabilistic data models for beam position; only deterministic, hard-bound calculations are acceptable for clinical treatment delivery.
key_technical_challenges: Compensating for the 'magnetic memory' effect (hysteresis) in superconducting gantry magnets during rapid rotation changes.
Synchronizing the millisecond-scale cyclotron extraction pulses with the mechanical rotation of a multi-ton gantry to within 0.1mm spatial accuracy.
Filtering high-voltage noise from the ionization chamber feedback loop without removing genuine beam current variance signals.

recommended_stack.language: Rust + one-line reason: Guarantees memory safety and zero-cost abstractions for hard real-time constraints.
recommended_stack.framework: PREEMPT-RT Linux Kernel + one-line reason: Provides deterministic latency for hardware interrupt handling.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB + one-line reason: Optimized for high-write time-series telemetry from beam sensors.
recommended_stack.message_broker: DDS (Data Distribution Service) + one-line reason: Industry standard for real-time, publish-subscribe communication in safety-critical systems.
